key_drivers: The dollar backdrop remains supportive for non-USD assets, with DXY at 98.087 and still lower over both the five-day and one-month windows.
Volatility conditions are constructive for tech: VIX stayed balanced at 17.87 while MOVE fell 3.01% and SOX rose 0.97%.
Macro growth signals are mixed rather than synchronized: GDPNow is unchanged at 1.3%, while China PMI remains above 50 but slowed from 52.1 to 50.8.
FX leadership is now more policy-driven than commodity-driven, with the ECB, RBA, and BoJ all still firmer than the neutral Fed backdrop in different ways.

stale_sources: CME FedWatch probabilities were not directly accessible in-session, so the neutral Fed classification uses the latest repository-verified same-week read.
GDPNow remains dated 2026-04-09 and is still the latest official Atlanta Fed update.
World Gold Council flow direction was confirmed for March 2026 update timing, but the detailed chart remains gated behind sign-in.
fallbacks_used: Used Trading Economics for copper because the designated Investing.com page was blocked in the browser environment.
Used the repository's latest verified EIA inventory classification for the current weekly petroleum balance after the landing page confirmed release freshness but not the detailed table.
Used the official ECB, RBA, and BoJ documents directly for policy-stance classification.
overnight_changes: DXY down 0.09% on the session and down 0.79% over five days.
VIX down 0.39% versus the prior close.

imminent: No fresh GDPNow release is scheduled until 2026-04-21.
Any weekend U.S.-Iran diplomacy headline can quickly reprice crude and the dollar.
near_term: Bank of Japan meeting on 2026-04-27 to 2026-04-28
background: March central-bank communication still favors non-USD FX relative to the Fed.
China activity is still expanding, but momentum is softer than in February.
